content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as both a passive physical phenomenon (light reflection) and an active deliberate cognitive act (critical reflection), LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to split into two complementary modes: a passive, continuous 'reflection' mode that mirrors and records inconsistencies across generated outputs, and an active, deliberate 'reflexion' mode that revises stored memory to resolve detected inconsistencies. This hybrid framework draws directly on the dual etymological and functional meaning of the term 'reflexion' to address gaps in current single-mode LLM self-reflection approaches.
